video.title: Giro d'Italia 2020 - Stage 21 Highlights | Cycling | Eurosport

transcript.clean_text: never had a grand tour been as close going into the final day two riders neck and neck nothing to separate them apart from a few hundredths of a second milan and 15.7 kilometers would decide all against the clock the gerald italia and the maya rosa in play stage 21 from chennusco sulnaviglio to milan victor cabanance would be one of the favorites for the victory 17 minutes 57 seconds have been the best time set when he finished he bested that by nine seconds our record holder still looking for his first ever grand tour stage win philippe organa had fallon winning both the time trials at a breakaway looking for stage win number four the big favor and he would not disappoint yet again the world time trial champion would go supersonic earning a dot for the cap from the man he was about to beat and he wouldn't just beat him he would smash his time out the water 32 seconds quicker ghana in the lead and unlikely to be beaten but the day would get better for the ineos grenadiers rowan dennis unable to beat his teammates time but still on the podium on the day and all the work he'd done in the mountains was about to pay off tailgate on the same time as leader jai hindley the australian and the brit both in their mid-20s both looking to win their first giro d'italia and both coming back after starting this race as domestique's from the midpoint it became clear that gagan hart was in the box seat putting in a very competitive time trial and already by this point pulling 20 odd seconds out on the west australian hinley coming across at the 10 kilometer time track almost 30 seconds down and does tailgate and heart road into milan it will be a very special moment previous best and a grand tour of 20th place and from all the way in east london the hackney marshes where he used to play football to the roads of milan where we would ride to a grand tour win hindi riding into the final kilometer gagan heart realizing a dream but a heartbreak for a maya rosa who only got to wear it on his back for 18 minutes 53 seconds 39 seconds would be the difference at the end and statistically this would be the ineos grenadiers best ever grand tour seven stage wins amalia bianca amaya could celebrate with his partner hannah barnes and celebrate two as felipe ghana won the stage seventh heaven for the ineos grenadiers ghana taking four whole stage wins gegenhart with two but the most important prize a grand tour win the team's fifth grand tour winner and only the second britain ever to win the never ending trophy for the winner of the giro d'italia from vin denson winning the first stage in 1966 to dale gegenhart the first london-born rider winning the geelo in 2020 wilco calderman would finish on the podium with chihuahua almeida moving up from fifth to fourth you
